name: Navaratri Devis
description: The Navaratri Devis are the nine manifestations of Goddess Durga, each worshipped during the nine nights of the Navaratri festival. These forms represent different aspects of Shakti, the divine feminine energy. The Navadurgas are Shailaputri, Brahmacharini, Chandraghanta, Kushmanda, Skandamata, Katyayani, Kalaratri, Mahagauri, and Siddhidhatri. Each goddess embodies unique powers and blessings, ranging from purity and devotion to courage and wisdom. Shailaputri, the daughter of the mountains, represents the beginning of the spiritual journey. Brahmacharini embodies austerity and devotion. Chandraghanta signifies peace and tranquility. Kushmanda is associated with the creation of the universe. Skandamata is the mother of Skanda (Kartikeya), representing maternal love and protection. Katyayani is known for her strength and ferocity in destroying evil. Kalaratri is the destroyer of ignorance and darkness. Mahagauri symbolizes purity and forgiveness. Siddhidhatri bestows supernatural powers and fulfills desires. Together, the Navaratri Devis represent the multifaceted nature of the divine feminine, offering guidance, protection, and ultimate liberation to devotees. Their worship during Navaratri is a celebration of the triumph of good over evil and the power of Shakti.
subtitle: Nine Forms of Goddess Durga

keyMyths.title: Shailaputri's Birth
keyMyths.description: Shailaputri, the daughter of the Himalayas, embodies nature and devotion. Her worship marks the beginning of Navaratri, symbolizing the start of a spiritual journey.

keyMyths.title: Kalaratri's Fierce Form
keyMyths.description: Kalaratri, with her dark complexion and fierce appearance, destroys ignorance and evil. She is a symbol of courage and the triumph of good over darkness.

keyMyths.title: Siddhidhatri's Blessings
keyMyths.description: Siddhidhatri grants supernatural powers and fulfills desires for her devotees. She represents the ultimate liberation and the attainment of spiritual perfection.
